title: BSA/AML Verification Skipped in 23% of New Account Calls
description: Agents are skipping the BSA/AML identity verification step in 23% of new account calls this week, up from a 4% baseline — a 5.75x spike. Pattern started April 17, correlating with a script update that moved verification from step 3 to step 7. Concentration on the evening shift where supervisor coverage is thinnest. 142 new accounts may have incomplete verification.

confidence.rationale: Skip rate is a direct read from process-adherence audit log. Account counts are deterministic CRM joins.

title: Repeat Contact Spike — 31% Increase for Bill Pay Issues
description: Members calling back within 48 hours increased 31% for bill pay issues. First-call resolution for bill pay dropped from 81% to 54%. Root cause: agents providing workaround instructions for a digital banking sync latency issue logged by IT on April 14. Workarounds fail, members call back. IT fix estimated April 25.

confidence.rationale: FCR drop and repeat-rate are direct telemetry; IT root-cause linkage anchored to Pearson 0.87 correlation across 9 days.

title: Top Performer Burnout Risk — 3 of Top 10 Agents Declining
description: 3 of your top 10 rated agents show declining quality scores over the last 30 days. Average handle time is increasing and positive sentiment ratio is decreasing. These 3 agents handle the highest-complexity calls in mortgage servicing. Loss of any of these agents would significantly impact quality metrics.

confidence.rationale: Burnout-pattern model has 0.79 precision against 24 prior cases; 60–90 day pre-resignation window is well-validated but agent-specific outcomes vary.
